meeting_context: Public hearing of the Joint Administrative Zoning and Subdivision Committee held on October 12, 2022. The meeting covered a consent calendar item at 2134 Old Middlefield Way, a two-part development application at 339 Franklin St., a one-year vesting tentative map extension at 1411-1495 W El Camino Real, and a lot line adjustment at 890 Central Ave. The committee approved the hearing items subject to findings and conditions and announced appeal windows where applicable.

points.text: Stephanie Williams opened the October 12, 2022 Joint Administrative Zoning and Subdivision Committee public hearing. No members of the public spoke during oral communications.
points.source: Stephanie Williams: So good afternoon and welcome to the the Joint Administrative Zoning and Subdivision Committee Public Hearing of Wednesday, October 12th, 2022. Stephanie Williams: Is there anyone from the public that would like to speak under oral communications? Stephanie Williams: All right, Not seeing anyone.
points.speaker: Stephanie Williams

points.text: The consent calendar item at 2134 Old Middlefield Way was approved without public comment, and Stephanie Williams stated that the item is appealable to the City Council within 10 calendar days.
points.source: Stephanie Williams: We have one item at 2134 Old Middlefield Way. Stephanie Williams: And not not seeing anyone wishing to speak on the consent calendar, So I will go ahead and approve the consent calendar and this item is appealable to the City Council within 10 calendar days.
points.speaker: Stephanie Williams

points.text: Staff presented a two-part application at 339 Franklin St. that includes a planned community permit, development review permit, heritage tree removal permit, and a lot line adjustment to combine two lots on a 0.17-acre site. Staff stated that the project is categorically exempt from CEQA and consistent with the P19 Downtown Precise Plan and the General Plan.
points.source: Ella Carraccian: So the project includes a request for a planned community permit and development review permit to construct to the 500 square foot single family home. Ella Carraccian: And the project also includes a heritage tree removal permit to remove one heritage tree and .17 acre project site. Ella Carraccian: And it is determination that the project is categorically exempt from the sequel review and also a lock line adjustment to remove an existing line and the side and the proposed project. Ella Carraccian: The Yeah is consistent with the P19 Downtown Precise Plan since the project supports the intent of Area B to transition from from downtown core to the one and two Storey single family homes surrounding neighbourhoods. Ella Carraccian: Precise Plan and the proposed project is consistent with the General Plan as it allows up to 35 dwelling units per acre.
points.speaker: Ella Carraccian

points.text: Staff said the heritage tree on the property line should be removed because its structure creates a substantial breakage risk and because foundation work would cause nearly 50 percent root loss, increasing instability and risk to adjacent homes.
points.source: Ella Carraccian: So, as mentioned, the project includes a heritage removal which is located exactly on the property line with the neighbour. Ella Carraccian: With just a neighbour on the right it is a very to remove the tree and due to health condition and a structure of the tree, the tree has kind of like 2 narrow angle crutches near the ground level that are at substantial risk for breakage. Ella Carraccian: At both sides of the crotches are kind of laying down which which is adding pressure to push the trunks apart and much tree failure would damage one or both adjacent homes. Ella Carraccian: And also it is necessary to remove the tree in order to construct and the improvements and on this property and as part of like because the foundation construction as a result of this project would result in nearly 50% root loss resulting in an increased tree instability which is kind of like dangerous for both neighbours.
points.speaker: Ella Carraccian

points.text: Applicant Jenny Gong supported the project and said the existing duplex is uninhabitable and not rentable because it lacks utilities, while a new two-story home would improve the neighborhood and community.
points.source: Jenny Gong: So my name is Jenny Gong. Jenny Gong: I'm the applicant for the project. Jenny Gong: The current duplex structure that's currently there on the property is not only it's inhabitable but it's not rentable given that as a lack of utility or anything that in in the current structure and making of a brand new structure, two-story home, it's going to improve the neighborhood as well as the community.
points.speaker: Jenny Gong

points.text: Stephanie Williams clarified that the 339 Franklin St. item had two parts: the development permits and tree removal were acted on by the zoning administrator, while the lot line adjustment was acted on by the subdivision committee. She approved the development portion subject to draft findings and recommended conditions of approval.
points.source: Stephanie Williams: This is a two-part two-part item. Stephanie Williams: The first part being the planned community permit, development review permit and heritage tree removal permit will just which is just acted on by the zoning administrator. Stephanie Williams: And then the second part being of the lot line adjustment acted by the subdivision committee. Stephanie Williams: I have reviewed the project as well as staff's recommendation, draft findings as well as recommended conditions of approval. Stephanie Williams: And we'll be approving the planned community permit, development review permit and heritage tree removal subject to those draft findings and conditions of approval.
points.speaker: Stephanie Williams

topics.topic: 1411-1495  West El Camino Real extension request

points.text: Staff presented a request for a one-year extension of a previously approved vesting tentative map at 1411-1495 W El Camino Real to create 53 condominium lots and one common lot on a 1.25-acre site. Staff said the extension is consistent with the El Camino Real Precise Plan and is categorically exempt from CEQA under Section 15332.
points.source: Brittany Whitehill: The applicant is requesting A1 year extension of a previously approved vesting tentative map to create 53 condominium lots and one condom common lot on an R or on a 1.25 acre project. Brittany Whitehill: Site staff has found that the proposed vesting tentative map extension is consistent with the El Camino real precise plan and mixed-use corridor general planned land use designation as well as the subdivision map. Brittany Whitehill: Act staff has also determined that the project is categorically exempt from Sequa pursuant to Section 15332 infill developments of the SEQUEL guidelines.
points.speaker: Brittany Whitehill

points.text: The applicant representative said the requested one-year extension would allow time to complete construction documents and obtain permits.
points.source: Steve Saray: Good afternoon. Steve Saray: This is Steve Surrey with the Lux Larva development. Steve Saray: But as Brittany mentioned, we're just requesting for a one year extension and so we can get our construction documents and permits in place.
points.speaker: Steve Surrey

points.text: The subdivision committee unanimously approved the one-year vesting tentative map extension for 1411-1495 W El Camino Real, and Stephanie Williams stated that the item is appealable to the City Council within 10 calendar days.
points.source: Diana Fizely: I don't have any questions, but I would like and I've reviewed the materials for this item, but I would like to make a motion to approve the one year extension of the vesting tentative map at 1411 to 1495 W El Camino Real. Ed Arango: I'll second that. Stephanie Williams: Unanimous approval from the subdivision committee. Stephanie Williams: This item is appealable to the City Council within 10 calendar days.
points.speaker: Stephanie Williams

points.text: Staff presented a lot line adjustment at 890 Central Ave., on the northeast corner of Sterling Rd. and Central Ave., to relocate a lot line between two existing lots on a 0.44-acre site. Staff said the proposal is consistent with R1 zoning and low-density residential land use standards and is categorically exempt from CEQA under Section 15305.
points.source: Brittany Whitehill: So this project is located at 890 Central Ave. Brittany Whitehill: which is on the northeast corner of Sterling Rd. Brittany Whitehill: And Central Ave. Brittany Whitehill: In the R1 Single Family Residential District, the applicant is requesting a lot line adjustment to relocate a lot line between two existing lots on a .44 acre project. Brittany Whitehill: Site staff has found the proposed lot line adjustment is consistent with the R1 zoning standards and low density residential general land use designation as well as the subdivision map. Brittany Whitehill: Act staff has also determined that the project is categorically exempt from Sequa pursuant to Section 15305 minor alterations in land use limitations of the Sequa guidelines.
points.speaker: Brittany Whitehill

points.text: The subdivision committee unanimously approved the 890 Central Ave. lot line adjustment subject to conditions of approval and conformance with the General Plan and Zoning Ordinance.
points.source: Ed Arango: I'd like to make a motion to approve the lot line adjustment at 890 Central, subject to the conditions of approval and conformance with the General Plan and Zoning Ordinance. Diana Fizely: I'll second that motion all. Stephanie Williams: So it's a unanimous approval by the subdivision committee.
points.speaker: Stephanie Williams

points.text: After completing the agenda, Stephanie Williams adjourned the meeting at 4:15 PM.
points.source: Stephanie Williams: And with that, we've reached the end of our agenda. Stephanie Williams: So we will adjourn this meeting at 4:15 PM.
points.speaker: Stephanie Williams

decisions.text: The zoning administrator approved the consent calendar item at 2134 Old Middlefield Way; Stephanie Williams stated that the item is appealable to the City Council within 10 calendar days.
decisions.source: Stephanie Williams: And not not seeing anyone wishing to speak on the consent calendar, So I will go ahead and approve the consent calendar and this item is appealable to the City Council within 10 calendar days.

decisions.text: The zoning administrator approved the planned community permit, development review permit, and heritage tree removal permit for 339 Franklin St. subject to draft findings and recommended conditions of approval.
decisions.source: Stephanie Williams: And we'll be approving the planned community permit, development review permit and heritage tree removal subject to those draft findings and conditions of approval.

decisions.text: The subdivision committee unanimously approved the lot line adjustment at 339 Franklin St. to combine two lots into one lot on the 0.17-acre project site, subject to conditions of approval and conformance with the General Plan and Zoning Ordinance.
decisions.source: Ed Arango: I did read the staff memo, the conditions of approval, and the plan, and I'd like to make a motion to approve the lot line adjustment to remove an existing lot line to combine two lots into one lot on .17 acre project site at 339 Franklin St. Ed Arango: subject to the conditions of approval and conformance with the General plan and Zoning Ordinance. Stephanie Williams: So that's a unanimous approval from the subdivision committee.

decisions.text: The subdivision committee unanimously approved the one-year extension of the vesting tentative map at 1411-1495 W El Camino Real.
decisions.source: Diana Fizely: I don't have any questions, but I would like and I've reviewed the materials for this item, but I would like to make a motion to approve the one year extension of the vesting tentative map at 1411 to 1495 W El Camino Real. Ed Arango: I'll second that. Stephanie Williams: Unanimous approval from the subdivision committee.

decisions.text: The subdivision committee unanimously approved the lot line adjustment at 890 Central Ave. subject to conditions of approval and conformance with the General Plan and Zoning Ordinance.
decisions.source: Ed Arango: I'd like to make a motion to approve the lot line adjustment at 890 Central, subject to the conditions of approval and conformance with the General Plan and Zoning Ordinance. Diana Fizely: I'll second that motion all. Stephanie Williams: So it's a unanimous approval by the subdivision committee.
